name: agent-ux
description: Use for AGENT UX — the interaction surface an AI agent acts THROUGH as an end user (a UI, app, or computer-use target), often on a human's behalf. DO — make a surface an agent can perceive, target, and act on safely. REVIEW — audit a surface for machine-readable state, deterministic actions, agent agency/approval, and human-vs-agent conflict, then score it. DESIGN — shape the machine-readable state, the stable action handles, the approval/authority gate, or the human/agent dual path, or explain a principle. Triggers on 'can our agent actually use this UI', 'make our app agent-operable', 'should the agent confirm this action', 'our agent clicks the wrong control', 'expose our app state for computer-use'. Do NOT use for human-only product UX or visual design (use ux-audit / ui-design), docs an agent reads (use agent-docs), the SDK/tool schema or token-exchange mechanism (use agent-dx), or operating the agent loop (use agent-ops).

inspired_by.name: WAI-ARIA (Accessible Rich Internet Applications)
inspired_by.author: W3C
inspired_by.contribution: The accessibility tree — roles, accessible names, and states — is the structured surface assistive tech and agents read instead of pixels, grounding machine-readable state and stable semantic handles for targeting controls.

inspired_by.name: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG)
inspired_by.author: W3C
inspired_by.contribution: Information conveyed through presentation must be programmatically determinable and not by color or sensory characteristics alone — the perceivability floor for an agent actor and the basis for the visible-plus-machine-readable dual path.

metadata.catalog_summary: Agent-as-end-user: the surface an AI agent acts through (a UI, app, or computer-use target), often for a human. Make it perceivable and safe to act on, audit it for machine-readable state and approval gates, or design the state, action handles, and human/agent dual path. The agent-actor analog of human UX.
